'''Mitchell Sargent''' (born 2 July 1979 in [[Canowindra, New South Wales]]), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Sarge"''', is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. A [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|Country New South Wales representative]] forward, he played club football in the [[NRL]] for the [[Melbourne Storm]], the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] and [[Newcastle Knights]] and in the [[Super League]] for English side, [[Castleford Tigers]].

==Playing career==
Sargent made his first grade debut for Melbourne in round 1 2002 against the [[Canberra Raiders]], scoring a try in a 16–12 victory at the [[Olympic Park Stadium (Melbourne)|Olympic Park Stadium]].

Sargent's final game for Melbourne was their 30-0 semi final defeat against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]] at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].  In 2004, Sargent signed for North Queensland and was part of the club's first ever finals campaign.  He played from the bench in North Queensland's preliminary final loss against the [[Sydney Roosters]] at [[Telstra Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/ready-roosters-end-cows-dream-run-20040927-gdjt7x.html/|title=Ready Roosters end Cows' dream run|website=SMH|date=27 September 2004 }}</ref>

Sargent played from the interchange bench in the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]' first ever [[2005 NRL grand final|grand final in 2005]] which was lost to the [[Wests Tigers]].

On 24 August 2006 Sargent had his contract with the North Queensland Cowboys terminated after he tested positive to [[cocaine]] from "in-house tests" conducted by the club.  According to a statement from Cowboys management, Sargent admitted using the drug and waived his right to a 'B' sample [http://www.smh.com.au/news/league/cocaine-test-traps-cowboy/2006/08/24/1156012658979.html].

National Rugby League Chief Executive [[David Gallop]] confirmed in the same report that Sargent would not face the normal mandatory two-year ban prescribed by the [[World Anti-Doping Agency]] as the positive result was from an in-house, out-of-competition test.

Although banished from the North Queensland Cowboys' team, Sargent was offered a three-year contract (starting in 2007) with the [[Newcastle Knights]], which he accepted [https://web.archive.org/web/20070311005240/http://nrl.com.au/Clubs/Knights/KnightsNewsArticle/tabid/119/NewsId/2897/Default.aspx]. He stated that he was "over the moon to be given this second chance".

He left the [[Newcastle Knights]] after signing a two and half-year deal with [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in 2008.<ref name="Sargent signs for Tigers">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/06/05/RUGBYL_Castleford.html|title=Sargent signs for Tigers|publisher=Sportinglife|date=5 June 2008|accessdate=2008-06-05}}</ref>

Sargent left Castleford at the end of 2010 and is now retired.

==Career highlights==
* ''NRL Debut:'' 2002 - Round 1, Storm v [[Canberra Raiders]], [[Olympic Park, Melbourne]]
* ''SL Debut:'' 2008 - Round ??, Castleford v Huddersfield Giants
